hero_subtitle: SFTY gives compliance leadership a provider-neutral control layer that connects onboarding reviews, audit evidence, and regulatory rules into a single operational workflow.
trust_card_1_value: Identity checks connected to onboarding decisions
trust_card_2_value: Corporate verification, UBO review and entity risk
trust_card_3_value: Wallet and transaction monitoring in one workflow
trust_card_4_value: Message routing, completeness checks and exception handling
trust_card_5_value: Regulatory changes mapped to internal rule
trust_card_6_value: Decision logs and evidence for every review
problem_title: Onboarding review slows with manual complexity
problem_description: Compliance teams face delays and duplicated effort when onboarding processes rely on disconnected checks and manual handoffs. This limits executive visibility and creates gaps in audit readiness and operational control as scaling pressures increase.
problem_point_1_value: KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule outputs sit in separate tools
problem_point_2_value: Teams move data between dashboards, spreadsheets and internal notes
problem_point_3_value: Regulatory changes are hard to map into internal workflows
problem_point_4_value: Evidence and decision history are difficult to reconstruct later
solution_description: SFTY integrates KYC, KYB and supporting provider outputs into one scalable onboarding workflow that preserves decision evidence and gives executives clear audit trails and operational visibility.
workflow_title: From onboarding checks to approval decisions
workflow_description: SFTY consolidates KYC and KYB provider results, applies risk-based routing, routes reviews and records approval decisions with supporting audit evidence.
workflow_step_1_description: KYC, KYB, KYT and screening outputs enter SFTY
workflow_step_2_description: Risk rules route checks into the right workflow
workflow_step_3_description: A case opens with customer, wallet and provider context
workflow_step_4_description: Teams review, decide and record the outcome
workflow_step_5_description: Logs, decisions and reports stay ready for audit
why_sfty_title: Not just another provider. The compliance control layer
why_sfty_description: SFTY delivers a provider-neutral layer that connects onboarding checks into audit-ready workflows, evidence, decisions and executive visibility without disrupting existing tools.
why_sfty_card_1_description: Work above existing KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule tools
why_sfty_card_2_description: Turn disconnected outputs into structured reviews and cases
why_sfty_card_3_description: Preserve evidence, reviewer actions and decision history
why_sfty_card_4_description: Give compliance, risk and leadership one operating view
authority_description: SFTY supports VASPs scaling onboarding operations with provider orchestration, audit-ready evidence and enterprise deployment for trusted bank due diligence and operational visibility.
authority_card_1_description: Connect KYC, KYB, KYT, Travel Rule and screening providers into one workflow
authority_card_2_description: Preserve provider outputs, reviewer notes, decisions and supporting records
authority_card_3_description: Prepare clearer compliance evidence for banks, partners and external review
authority_card_4_description: Support private environments, permissions, integrations and internal governance
ask_sfty_placeholder: How can SFTY support scalable onboarding workflows for VASPs
ask_sfty_chip_1_message: Explain how SFTY structures onboarding review and decision logs
ask_sfty_chip_2_message: Show how audit-ready evidence is preserved during onboarding reviews
ask_sfty_chip_3_message: Describe how SFTY connects provider outputs for compliance control
ask_sfty_helper_text: AI assistant for product, deployment and compliance questions
faq_description: Clear answers on provider orchestration, Travel Rule workflows, audit evidence and enterprise compliance operations.
faq_1_question: What is SFTY for onboarding risk control?
faq_1_answer: SFTY provides a compliance layer that structures onboarding reviews, links identity, wallet and risk data, and supports audit-ready evidence for regulated digital asset businesses.
faq_2_question: How does SFTY scale onboarding operations?
faq_2_answer: SFTY connects provider outputs into workflows, automates review routing, applies internal rules, and consolidates case management to reduce manual workload and maintain control during onboarding scale-up.
faq_3_question: How does SFTY preserve audit-ready evidence?
faq_3_answer: SFTY preserves decision logs, provider results, case notes and evidence packages to support audit readiness and demonstrate clear compliance workflows throughout onboarding and risk review.
faq_4_question: Can SFTY integrate with existing compliance stacks?
faq_4_answer: Yes. SFTY sits above KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule providers and integrates their outputs without disrupting existing systems or requiring provider replacement.
faq_5_question: Does SFTY replace onboarding or KYC providers?
faq_5_answer: No. SFTY complements existing onboarding and KYC providers by turning their outputs into structured workflows, decisions, evidence and reporting without replacing them.
final_description: Review how SFTY connects onboarding reviews, provider outputs and evidence into a controlled compliance operation for VASPs.
footer_description: Supervisory and compliance control layer for digital asset markets, businesses and regulated institutions.
footer_contact_title: For supervisory, compliance and deployment discussions.
footer_legal_notice: This page is for informational purposes only and does not constitute legal, regulatory or compliance advice. SFTY deployment depends on jurisdiction, client requirements and applicable laws.
